messages.content:
I seem to only be able to have either or.
  
[Unit]
Description=GTG Next (Next.js via PM2 cluster) - wait for Docker; build then start
Wants=docker.service network-online.target
After=docker.service network-online.target
[Service]
Type=simple
User=root
Group=root
# Match your interactive PM2 environment
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2
Environment=HOME=/root
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=3000
Environment=PNPM_HOME=/root/.local/share/pnpm
Environment=PATH=/root/.local/share/pnpm:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
WorkingDirectory=/mnt/gtg-staging/runtime/golf-travel-group/apps/next
# Wait for dependent containers (adjust names if needed)
# ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-app-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-app-1..."; sleep 2; done'
# ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-db-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-db-1..."; sleep 2; done'
# ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if docker inspect golf-travel-group-wordpress-1 >/dev/null 2>&1; then until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-wordpress-1)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-wordpress-1..."; sleep 2; done; fi'
# ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if docker inspect golf-travel-group-mailpit-1 >/dev/null 2>&1; then until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-mailpit-1)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-mailpit-1..."; sleep 2; done; fi'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'name=golf-travel-group-wordpress-1; echo "Waiting for $name to be healthy..."; until docker inspect "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done; until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" "$name")" = "true" ]; do sleep 1; done; until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{if .State.Health}}{{.State.Health.Status}}{{else}}none{{end}}" "$name")" = "healthy" ]; do sleep 2; done; echo "$name is healthy."'
# Ensure prerequisites
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'command -v node >/dev/null || { echo "node not found"; exit 1; }'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'command -v pm2 >/dev/null || { echo "pm2 not found (install with: npm i -g pm2)"; exit 1; }'
# Install deps if missing, then build if needed
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if [ ! -d node_modules ]; then if command -v pnpm >/dev/null; then pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm install; elif command -v corepack >/dev/null; then corepack enable pnpm && corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || corepack pnpm install; else npm ci || npm i; fi; fi'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if [ ! -d .next ] || [ ! -f .next/BUILD_ID ]; then if command -v pnpm >/dev/null; then pnpm run build; elif command -v corepack >/dev/null; then corepack pnpm run build; else npm run build; fi; fi'
# Avoid name clash if previously started
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
#ExecStartPre=/usr/local/bin/gtg-healthcheck.sh
# Run exactly like manual launch but via pm2-runtime so systemd tracks the process
ExecStart=/bin/bash -lc 'PORT=$PORT pm2-runtime start node_modules/next/dist/bin/next --name gtg-next -i max -- start -p ${PORT}'
ExecStop=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
TimeoutStartSec=20min
SyslogIdentifier=gtg-next
# Hardening (keep, but allow /root home for PM2)
NoNewPrivileges=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
ProtectSystem=full
ProtectHome=false
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
  
  
if gtg-next comes up then it kills gtg-next-gtc
  
[Unit]
Description=GTG Next-GTC (Next.js via PM2 cluster) - wait for Docker; build then start
Wants=docker.service network-online.target
After=docker.service network-online.target
[Service]
Type=simple
User=root
Group=root
# Match interactive PM2 env
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2
Environment=HOME=/root
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=3001
Environment=HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0
Environment=PNPM_HOME=/root/.local/share/pnpm
Environment=PATH=/root/.local/share/pnpm:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
# CHANGE this if your app folder is different
Environment=NEXT_GTC_DIR=/mnt/gtg-staging/runtime/golf-travel-group/apps/next-gtc
# Keep WorkingDirectory at monorepo root for pnpm workspace ops
WorkingDirectory=/mnt/gtg-staging/runtime/golf-travel-group
# Wait for required containers to be running
#ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-wordpress-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-wordpress-1..."; sleep 2; done'
#ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-app-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-app-1..."; sleep 2; done'
#ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-mailpit-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-mailpit-1..."; sleep 2; done'
#ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-wordpress-mitm-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-wordpress-mitm-1..."; sleep 2; done'
#ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" golf-travel-group-db-1 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ]; do echo "Waiting for golf-travel-group-db-1..."; sleep 2; done'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'name=golf-travel-group-wordpress-1; echo "Waiting for $name to be healthy..."; until docker inspect "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done; until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{.State.Running}}" "$name")" = "true" ]; do sleep 1; done; until [ "$(docker inspect -f "{{if .State.Health}}{{.State.Health.Status}}{{else}}none{{end}}" "$name")" = "healthy" ]; do sleep 2; done; echo "$name is healthy."'
# Ensure pnpm (or corepack -> pnpm), Node, PM2
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if command -v pnpm >/dev/null; then pnpm -v; elif command -v corepack >/dev/null; then corepack enable pnpm && corepack pnpm -v; else echo "Neither pnpm nor corepack found on PATH"; exit 1; fi'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'command -v node >/dev/null || { echo "node not found"; exit 1; }'
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'command -v pm2 >/dev/null || { echo "pm2 not found (npm i -g pm2)"; exit 1; }'
# Sanity: app dir exists
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc '[ -d "${NEXT_GTC_DIR}" ] || { echo "NEXT_GTC_DIR not found: ${NEXT_GTC_DIR}"; exit 1; }'
# Install deps for next-gtc if missing
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if [ ! -d "${NEXT_GTC_DIR}/node_modules" ]; then if command -v pnpm >/dev/null; then pnpm --filter next-gtc install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm --filter next-gtc install; elif command -v corepack >/dev/null; then corepack enable pnpm && (corepack pnpm --filter next-gtc install --frozen-lockfile || corepack pnpm --filter next-gtc install); else (cd "${NEXT_GTC_DIR}" && (npm ci || npm i)); fi; fi'
# Build only the GTC app before serving
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'if command -v pnpm >/dev/null; then pnpm --filter next-gtc build; else corepack pnpm --filter next-gtc build; fi'
# Clear any stale PM2 process
ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next-gtc >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
# Start: prefer standalone server if present; else `next start -p 3001`
ExecStart=/bin/bash -lc 'cd "${NEXT_GTC_DIR}" && if [ -f ".next/standalone/server.js" ]; then PORT="${PORT}" HOSTNAME="${HOSTNAME}" pm2-runtime start .next/standalone/server.js --name gtg-next-gtc -i max; else PORT="${PORT}" pm2-runtime start node_modules/next/dist/bin/next --name gtg-next-gtc -i max -- start -p "${PORT}"; fi'
ExecStop=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next-gtc >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
TimeoutStartSec=20min
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=gtg-next-gtc
# Hardening (allow PM2 to write to /root/.pm2)
NoNewPrivileges=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
ProtectSystem=full
ProtectHome=false
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Great — it looks like you have two separate `systemd` units (`gtg-next.service` and `gtg-next-gtc.service`), each of which launches a Next.js app via **PM2**, but you’ve hit the problem where *starting one kills the other*.  

That symptom means both services are sharing the **same PM2 process namespace** and both use **pm2-runtime with overlapping process management**, so when the second one starts, it forcibly deletes or replaces the one from the first unit.

Let’s go step‑by‑step to fix this.

---

## 🧩 Why This Happens

`pm2-runtime` uses `$PM2_HOME` (default: `/root/.pm2`) as its local process registry.  
In both of your units you have:

```ini
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2
```

and

```bash
pm2 delete gtg-next >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
pm2 delete gtg-next-gtc >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
```

When `pm2-runtime` starts in one service, it assumes it *owns* the entire system‐level PM2 daemon for that `$PM2_HOME`.   
So when another instance of `pm2-runtime` starts (same `$PM2_HOME`), it clears or overwrites the process list — killing whatever was launched by the previous service.

---

## ✅ Solution: Separate PM2 Namespaces

Each systemd service needs its own isolated PM2 process store.

### Option 1 — Use Different `$PM2_HOME` directories

Give each service its own PM2_HOME and logs, e.g.:

**gtg-next.service**
```ini
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2/gtg-next
```

**gtg-next-gtc.service**
```ini
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2/gtg-next-gtc
```

That way, the process registry files won’t collide.

Keep the `pm2 delete` statements (safe, they’ll only affect their own registry).  

---

### Option 2 — Use `pm2-runtime --namespace`

If you prefer sharing one `$PM2_HOME` but keeping processes separate, PM2 supports per‑namespace isolation:

```bash
pm2-runtime start ... --name gtg-next --namespace gtg-next
```

and

```bash
pm2-runtime start ... --name gtg-next-gtc --namespace gtg-next-gtc
```

This prevents one namespace from managing or killing the other’s processes, even under the same `$PM2_HOME`.

---

### Option 3 — Use Different Unix Users

Another clean isolation route:

- run `gtg-next` as user `next`
- run `gtg-next-gtc` as user `nextgtc`

Each user naturally has a separate home and `$PM2_HOME`.

But if running as root is necessary for path or Docker dependencies, stick to Option 1 or 2.

---

## 🔧 Example Updated Unit (Option 1)

```ini
[Unit]
Description=GTG Next (Next.js via PM2 cluster)
After=network-online.target docker.service
Wants=network-online.target docker.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=root
Group=root
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2/gtg-next
Environment=HOME=/root
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=3000
Environment=PNPM_HOME=/root/.local/share/pnpm
Environment=PATH=/root/.local/share/pnpm:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
WorkingDirectory=/mnt/gtg-staging/runtime/golf-travel-group/apps/next

ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'
ExecStart=/bin/bash -lc 'PORT=$PORT pm2-runtime start node_modules/next/dist/bin/next --name gtg-next -i max -- start -p ${PORT}'
ExecStop=/bin/bash -lc 'pm2 delete gtg-next >/dev/null 2>&1 || true'

Restart=always
RestartSec=5
SyslogIdentifier=gtg-next
NoNewPrivileges=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
ProtectSystem=full
ProtectHome=false

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

And in `gtg-next-gtc.service`:

```ini
Environment=PM2_HOME=/root/.pm2/gtg-next-gtc
```

---

## ⚙️ Verification

After updating both units:

```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart gtg-next.service
sudo systemctl restart gtg-next-gtc.service
```

Then check:

```bash
sudo systemctl status gtg-next
sudo systemctl status gtg-next-gtc
pm2 ls --pm2-home /root/.pm2/gtg-next
pm2 ls --pm2-home /root/.pm2/gtg-next-gtc
```

You should see both clusters independently managed and running at once.

---

## ✅ Summary

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|----------|--------|-----|
| Starting one kills the other | Shared `PM2_HOME` registry and single process list | Give each service its own `PM2_HOME`, or use `--namespace` |

---

Would you like me to show an example using the `--namespace` approach instead (keeps all logs under one `/root/.pm2` but isolates process names)?
